en.editorNote: Send to the friend who fakes a cold to skip family dinners during marriage-pressure season — and to the friend currently 'getting married just to get married,' to give them three seconds to reconsider
variations: Short: it's not that I don't want to get married — I don't want to get married just to answer 'why aren't you married yet.'
Advanced: other people's anxiety isn't your life plan. The aunt will have new questions next year. The marriage is the one you have to live in.

items.sourceNote: 韓劇傷疤主題經典金句「Scars are proof you survived」延伸，原創成長傷痕觀察

zh-tw.content:
韓劇有一句被引用一萬次的台詞：

「傷疤不是失敗，
是你活下來的證據。」

你身上的傷疤，
有些是看得到的——
小時候摔車的膝蓋、
切菜失手的食指、
動完手術的肚子。

有些是看不到的——
那段你沒跟任何人說的感情、
那次差點放棄的低潮、
那個你以為會變家人、結果連朋友都不是了的人。

以前你以為這些都是「壞掉的證據」。

你怕別人看見。
你想盡辦法遮起來。
你覺得有傷疤的人，是不完整的。

但有一天你會懂——

傷疤是你身體說的話：
「這裡受過傷，但我把它修好了。」

會留下傷疤的，
從來不是那些把你打倒的事，
是那些你「站起來繼續走」的事。

沒有傷疤的人有兩種：
一種是從來沒受過傷，
一種是還沒癒合完。

你身上的每一道疤，
都是你跟時間打過架、然後贏了的紀錄。

下次你覺得自己「不夠完整」的時候，
摸一摸那些疤——

那不是缺陷。
那是勳章。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在因為過去某段感情、某次失敗、某個遺憾而覺得自己「壞掉了」的朋友——他不是壞掉，他是已經修好了
variations: 短版：傷疤不是失敗，是你活下來的證據
進階版：你身上的每一道疤，都是你跟時間打過架、然後贏了的紀錄

en.content:
There's a K-drama line that's been quoted ten thousand times:

"Scars aren't failure.
They're proof that you survived."

The scars on your body —
some are visible:
the knee from falling off your bike as a kid,
the finger from a kitchen knife slip,
the stomach from that surgery.

Some aren't:
the relationship you never told anyone about,
the low you almost didn't make it through,
the person you thought would become family and ended up not even a friend.

You used to think these were "proof of being broken."

You were afraid people would see them.
You tried everything to cover them up.
You thought scarred people were incomplete people.

Then one day you understand —

a scar is your body talking:
"Something hurt here. I fixed it."

What leaves scars
was never the thing that knocked you down.
It was the part where you got back up and kept walking.

There are only two kinds of people without scars:
those who've never been hurt,
and those still healing.

Every scar on you
is a record of a fight you had with time —
and won.

Next time you feel "not complete enough,"
touch the scars —

that isn't damage.
That's a medal.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still convinced they're 'broken' because of an old relationship, an old failure, an old regret — they're not broken. They're already healed.
variations: Short: scars aren't failure. They're proof you survived.
Advanced: every scar on you is a record of a fight you had with time — and won.

items.sourceNote: 2026陸劇《逐玉》全球爆紅，封神金句「我殺豬養你」延伸，原創愛情承諾觀察

zh-tw.content:
《逐玉》最讓人哭爆的，不是樊長玉的武功，
是她對謝征說的那句：

「你若留下，我殺豬養你。」

沒有玫瑰。
沒有鑽戒。
沒有「我會給你全世界」。

只有一個女人，
用自己最會的那件事——殺豬，
換一個她願意守一輩子的人。

後來你才懂——

最動人的愛情誓言，
從來不是「我會努力變更好」，
是「我用我現在的樣子，認真養你」。

會說「我給你最好的」的人很多。
會說「我用我有的，全部給你」的人很少。

前者是條件。
後者是決心。

如果有一天有人對你說：
「你若留下，我用我所有的，把你護好。」

不要懷疑。

那是這輩子你可能聽過最浪漫的話——
它沒有包裝，
但它有重量。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在等「條件齊全」的對象出現的朋友——真正的愛情從來不是談條件，是兩個人願意把現在的自己全部交出去
variations: 短版：最動人的愛情誓言不是「我會變更好」，是「我用我現在的樣子認真養你」
進階版：會說『我給你最好的』的人很多，會說『我用我有的全部給你』的人很少。前者是條件，後者是決心

en.content:
The line from 2026 hit drama Pursuit of Jade that wrecked everyone
wasn't about martial arts.
It was what Fan Changyu said to Xie Zheng:

"If you stay, I'll slaughter pigs to feed you."

No roses.
No diamond ring.
No "I'll give you the world."

Just a woman
using the one thing she's actually good at — butchering pigs —
to offer someone she's willing to keep for a lifetime.

Later you understand —

the most moving love vow
was never "I'll work on becoming better."
It's "I'll take care of you with exactly who I am right now."

Lots of people will say "I'll give you the best."
Few will say "I'll give you everything I have."

The first is a condition.
The second is a commitment.

If someone ever says to you:
"If you stay, I'll protect you with everything I have."

Don't doubt it.

That may be the most romantic sentence you'll ever hear —
no packaging,
but full of weight.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still waiting for the 'fully qualified' partner — real love was never about meeting conditions, it's two people willing to hand over who they are right now
variations: Short: the most moving love vow isn't 'I'll become better' — it's 'I'll take care of you with who I am right now.'
Advanced: many people say 'I'll give you the best.' Few say 'I'll give you everything I have.' The first is a condition. The second is commitment.

items.sourceNote: 2026 Netflix台劇《乩身》全球熱播，「他逃得了法律，逃得了報應嗎？」延伸，原創因果觀察

zh-tw.content:
《乩身》第八集那句話，看完整夜沒睡：

「他逃得了法律，
逃得了報應嗎？」

你身邊也有那種人——

他傷了一個人，
沒人追究。

他騙了一筆錢，
沒人發現。

他踩著別人往上爬，
沒人攔得住。

你看著他活得好好的，
甚至比那些被他傷過的人還要光鮮。

你忍不住懷疑：
難道好人真的活該倒楣，
壞人就可以一路順風？

但你再多看幾年——

你會發現他笑容裡的疲憊。
你會看見他半夜失眠的眼神。
你會聽見他身邊的人一個一個離開。

報應從來不是天打雷劈。

是他每天照鏡子，
知道自己是誰，
卻沒辦法跟自己和好。

是他擁有越多，
越怕失去；
身邊越熱鬧，
越覺得空。

法律會放過他。
時間不會。

你不用親手討回什麼。
你只要好好活著——

看著他用一輩子，
慢慢還他欠自己的那筆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在為「壞人怎麼可以這麼好命」憤憤不平的朋友——別氣，時間會代你出手
variations: 短版：報應從來不是天打雷劈，是他每天照鏡子卻沒辦法跟自己和好
進階版：法律會放過他，時間不會。你不用親手討回什麼，看著他用一輩子慢慢還他欠自己的那筆

en.content:
From episode 8 of 2026 Netflix hit Tang-ki — the line that kept everyone awake:

"He escaped the law.
Can he escape karma?"

You know someone like this —

he hurt someone,
no one held him accountable.

he took money,
no one caught him.

he stepped on others to climb,
no one stopped him.

You watch him living fine,
better even than the people he wrecked.

You can't help wondering:
are good people really doomed to suffer
while bad people just sail through?

Then you watch a few more years —

you notice the exhaustion in his smile.
you see the look in his eyes at 3 a.m.
you hear about the people leaving his life one by one.

Karma was never lightning from the sky.

It's him looking in the mirror every day,
knowing exactly who he is,
unable to make peace with himself.

It's the more he owns,
the more he fears losing;
the louder his life,
the emptier he feels.

The law will let him off.
Time won't.

You don't have to collect anything yourself.
Just live well —

and watch him spend a lifetime
slowly paying back what he owes himself.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still seething over 'how can bad people have it so good' — don't waste your anger. Time will collect for you.
variations: Short: karma was never lightning from the sky. It's him looking in the mirror every day, unable to make peace with himself.
Advanced: the law will let him off. Time won't. You don't have to collect anything — just live well and watch him pay himself back.

items.sourceNote: 韓劇《雖然是精神病但沒關係》「沒有所謂好人」金句延伸，原創成年人選擇觀察
tags: 雖然是精神病但沒關係

zh-tw.content:
韓劇《雖然是精神病但沒關係》有一句話，
被很多三十歲後的人重新存了一次：

「沒有所謂的好人。
只有在每個當下，
做出最不糟選擇的人。」

小時候你以為人分兩種：
好人，壞人。

長大才發現——

所謂好人，
不過是在不被看見的時候，
還願意選擇善良的人。

所謂善良，
不過是明明可以計較，
但決定算了。

所謂強大，
不過是知道自己會被辜負，
還是先給出溫柔。

沒有人天生是好人。

他們只是——

在每一個可以變壞的瞬間，
選擇了不變壞。

在每一個可以報復的機會，
選擇了放過。

在每一個可以冷漠的時刻，
選擇了多走一步。

你以為的「好人」，
其實是一個一個小選擇堆出來的。

而那些選擇，
沒有人會替他鼓掌，
也沒有人會給他獎牌。

他就是默默地，
每天做一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個常常覺得自己「不夠好」的朋友——你不是天生善良，你是每天都在選擇善良，那比天生厲害多了
variations: 短版：沒有天生的好人，只有每個當下做出最不糟選擇的人
進階版：所謂善良不過是明明可以計較但決定算了；所謂強大不過是知道會被辜負還是先給出溫柔

en.content:
From the K-drama It's Okay to Not Be Okay,
a line everyone over thirty has re-saved at least once:

"There's no such thing as a good person.
Only people who, in each moment,
make the least bad choice."

As a kid you thought people came in two types:
good, bad.

Then you grew up —

so-called good people
are just the ones who choose kindness
when no one is watching.

So-called kindness
is just deciding to let it go
when you absolutely could have held a grudge.

So-called strength
is knowing you'll be let down
and offering tenderness anyway.

No one is born good.

They just —

in every moment they could've turned cruel,
chose not to.

in every chance to get even,
chose to let it go.

in every moment they could've stayed cold,
took one more step.

The "good person" you see
is actually built one small choice at a time.

And for those choices,
no one claps,
no one hands out medals.

They just quietly
make them every day.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps thinking they're 'not good enough' — you're not naturally good. You choose good every single day. That's way more impressive than being born that way.
variations: Short: no one is born good. There are only people who, in each moment, make the least bad choice.
Advanced: kindness is deciding to let it go when you could have held a grudge. Strength is offering tenderness even knowing you'll be let down.

items.sourceNote: 台灣八點檔鄉土劇經典名台詞文化現象延伸，原創台灣人語感觀察

zh-tw.content:
你以為八點檔台詞很誇張？
其實它說的是台灣人的內心戲：

「我跟你說，這件代誌沒完。」
→ 我嘴上說好，但我有記下來。

「你給我聽好，從今以後——」
→ 我已經在心裡想了三天怎麼開口。

「天公伯啊！怎麼會這樣！」
→ 我的 deadline 還有兩小時。

「我是看在你父親的面子上。」
→ 不然我才不想理你。

「你不要逼我做出我會後悔的事。」
→ 我已經打了三遍那段話又刪掉。

「你是不是把我當塑膠？」
→ 你已讀不回三天了。

「這個家，沒有我容身的地方了。」
→ 我今天加班到十一點才回家。

八點檔之所以演了三十年沒退流行，
不是因為劇情狗血，
是因為每一句台詞，
你阿嬤、你媽媽、你自己——

都默默在心裡演過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給家庭群組——讓阿嬤媽媽們知道你終於懂八點檔了，也讓他們知道你最近其實也在演那一齣
variations: 「你給我記住」= 我不會忘記，但我也不會再提，直到時機成熟
進階版：八點檔之所以三十年沒退流行，是因為每一句台詞你阿嬤、你媽媽、你自己都默默在心裡演過

en.content:
You think 8 p.m. Taiwanese soap dialogue is over the top?
It's actually just narrating the inner monologue of every Taiwanese person:

"I'm telling you, this isn't over."
→ I said okay out loud, but I wrote it down.

"You listen to me. From this day forward—"
→ I've been rehearsing this opening line for three days.

"Heavens above! How could this happen!"
→ My deadline is in two hours.

"I'm only doing this out of respect for your father."
→ Otherwise I wouldn't even talk to you.

"Don't push me into doing something I'll regret."
→ I've typed and deleted that message three times.

"Am I plastic to you?"
→ You've left me on read for three days.

"There's no place for me in this family anymore."
→ I worked until 11 p.m. again tonight.

These soaps have aired for thirty years and still aren't going out of style.
Not because the plots are insane.
Because every single line
your grandma, your mom, and you

have silently performed in your own head.
en.editorNote: Send to the family chat — let grandma and mom know you finally get the 8 p.m. soaps, and let them know you're starring in one of those scenes right now
variations: "You remember this" = I won't forget, but I won't bring it up either, until the time is right
Advanced: these soaps lasted thirty years not because the plots are crazy, but because every line is one your grandma, your mom, and you have silently performed in your own head

items.sourceNote: 韓劇《請回答1988》「家人是不會問理由的」金句延伸，原創家人關係觀察

zh-tw.content:
《請回答1988》最近又被翻出來——

德善爸爸那句話，
二十幾歲看是台詞，
三十幾歲看是真理：

「家人，
是不會問你為什麼的人。

他們只會問你，
餓不餓。」

你跟朋友吵架，朋友會問：
「所以到底是誰的錯？」

你跟同事不合，同事會問：
「你做了什麼讓他這樣？」

你失戀，前任會問：
「你有沒有想過自己哪裡可以改？」

只有家人——

你失業回家，
他什麼都不問，
冰箱打開，
微波一碗剩飯給你。

你深夜哭著回家，
他什麼都不問，
默默把熱水器打開，
說「先去洗澡」。

你三十歲還沒結婚，
阿姨會問。
爸媽會嘮叨。

但真的看見你過得不好的那一刻——

他們會閉嘴，
只剩一句：

「你要不要吃點什麼？」

那一句「餓不餓」，
不是在問你的胃，
是在問你——

「我能怎麼幫你？」

他們不會表白。
他們只會煮一鍋湯，
靜靜地放在你面前。

那就是這輩子，
你能收到最深的愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個跟家人關係有點僵的朋友——也提醒你自己，下次回家別只看手機，那一句「餓不餓」就是他能說出口最大的「我愛你」
variations: 短版：家人是不會問你為什麼的人，他們只會問你餓不餓
進階版：那一句『餓不餓』不是在問你的胃，是在問『我能怎麼幫你』

en.content:
Reply 1988 keeps getting re-shared lately —

Deok-sun's father had one line
that sounded like script in your twenties
and sounds like truth in your thirties:

"Family
are the people who don't ask you why.

They only ask you
if you're hungry."

When you fight with a friend, the friend asks:
"Whose fault was it, really?"

When you clash with a coworker, the coworker asks:
"What did you do to make him act like that?"

When you get dumped, the ex asks:
"Have you thought about what you could change?"

Only family —

you come home unemployed,
they ask nothing,
just open the fridge
and microwave you a bowl of leftover rice.

you come home crying at 2 a.m.,
they ask nothing,
just quietly turn on the water heater
and say "go shower first."

You're thirty and not married,
the aunts ask.
the parents nag.

But the moment they actually see you're not okay —

they shut up,
and all that's left is:

"Do you want to eat something?"

That "are you hungry"
isn't asking your stomach.
It's asking —

"How can I help you?"

They won't say it out loud.
They'll just cook a pot of soup
and quietly set it in front of you.

And that
may be the deepest love
you'll ever receive in this life.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's gone cold with family — and remind yourself, next time you go home, put the phone down. That 'are you hungry' is the biggest 'I love you' they know how to say out loud.
variations: Short: family are the people who don't ask you why. They only ask if you're hungry.
Advanced: that 'are you hungry' isn't asking your stomach. It's asking 'how can I help you?'

items.sourceNote: 2026治癒系韓劇金句「你的幸福為什麼要問別人」延伸，原創成年人自我對話觀察

zh-tw.content:
2026年最被存到手機備忘錄裡的一句韓劇台詞：

「你的幸福，
為什麼要問別人？」

你買了新衣服，先傳給朋友問好不好看。
你換了工作，先去看親戚臉色。
你決定一個人去旅行，還在猶豫媽會不會生氣。
你終於想分手，先問閨蜜：「我是不是太狠？」

從什麼時候開始，
你的每一個決定，
都要先過別人那一關？

劇裡那個三十歲的女主角，
在巷口的便利商店蹲著吃飯糰，
哭著說出這句話的時候——

我突然懂了。

所謂長大，
不是把自己活成大家滿意的版本。

是有一天，
你終於敢說：

「這件事我自己決定就好。」

而且不需要任何人按讚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個事事問人意見的朋友——你的人生不是要做給誰看的，幸福不該需要評審
variations: 短版：你的幸福，為什麼要問別人？
進階版：所謂長大不是把自己活成大家滿意的版本，是有一天你終於敢說『這件事我自己決定就好』

en.content:
The K-drama line most saved into people's phone notes in 2026:

"Your happiness —
why are you asking other people?"

You buy a new shirt and send it to a friend to vet first.
You change jobs and read your relatives' faces before mentioning it.
You plan a solo trip and still wonder if mom will be mad.
You finally want to break up and ask your best friend, "Am I being too harsh?"

When did it start —
that every single decision in your life
has to pass someone else's checkpoint first?

The thirty-something lead in the show,
crouched outside a convenience store eating a rice ball,
delivered that line through tears —

and it clicked.

Growing up isn't
becoming the version of yourself that everyone approves of.

It's the day
you can finally say:

"This one, I decide on my own."

And you don't need anyone to like the post.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who polls everyone before making any choice — your life isn't a performance. Happiness doesn't need a panel of judges.
variations: Short: your happiness — why are you asking other people?
Advanced: growing up isn't becoming the version of yourself everyone approves of. It's the day you can finally say 'this one, I decide on my own.'

items.sourceNote: 台劇《我們與惡的距離》宋喬安「不想一輩子當被害者家屬」金句延伸，原創傷痛與身份觀察

zh-tw.content:
《我們與惡的距離》最被記住的，
不是劇情有多刺，
是宋喬安那一句：

「我不想一輩子，
當被害者家屬。」

短短一句話，
卡在很多人喉嚨裡好幾年。

那個被劈腿的——
不想一輩子當「曾經被劈腿的人」。
那個被裁員的——
不想一輩子當「上一份工作被開除的人」。
那個家裡出事的——
不想一輩子當「那個家裡出過事的小孩」。

你以為的療癒，
是把事情忘掉。

後來發現——

真正的療癒，
是事情還在，
但它不再是你身分證上的第一行字。

你受過傷，
但你不是傷口本身。

你經歷過那件事，
但那件事不是你的姓名。

當有一天你介紹自己的時候，
不再從那件事開始——

你就贏了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直被過去定義的朋友——你經歷過那件事，但那件事不是你的姓名
variations: 短版：我不想一輩子，當被害者家屬
進階版：你受過傷，但你不是傷口本身；你經歷過那件事，但那件事不是你的姓名

en.content:
The most-remembered line from The World Between Us
isn't the most painful plot beat.
It's Song Qiao'an saying:

"I don't want to spend my whole life
being 'the victim's family.'"

One sentence,
stuck in a lot of people's throats for years.

The one who got cheated on —
doesn't want to spend forever being 'the one who got cheated on.'
The one who got laid off —
doesn't want to spend forever being 'the one fired from their last job.'
The one whose family went through something —
doesn't want to spend forever being 'the kid from that family.'

You thought healing
meant forgetting the thing.

Then you learned —

real healing
is that the thing is still there,
but it's no longer the first line on your ID.

You were hurt,
but you are not the wound.

You went through that thing,
but that thing is not your name.

The day you introduce yourself
and don't start with that story —

you've won.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend still defined by their past — you went through that thing, but that thing is not your name
variations: Short: I don't want to spend my whole life being 'the victim's family.'
Advanced: you were hurt, but you are not the wound. You went through that thing, but that thing is not your name.

items.sourceNote: 韓劇《低谷醫生》「人生不會永遠在低谷」金句延伸，原創低潮期成年人觀察

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》朴信惠那句被截圖傳爆：

「人生不會永遠在低谷。

但低谷的時候，
要先學會的不是爬出來——

是坐下來。」

你在低潮的時候，
所有人都跟你說：
「加油！」
「想開一點！」
「別人比你慘！」

沒有人告訴你——

你可以先坐下來。

你可以把那杯泡了三小時的咖啡喝完。
你可以把那部看到第五遍的劇再看一次。
你可以一整天什麼都不做，
只是看著天花板。

不要急著「振作」。

振作這兩個字，
是別人對你的期待，
不是你對自己的責任。

低谷不可怕。

可怕的是——

你連蹲下來休息一下，
都覺得自己有罪。

蹲下來，是為了下一次起跳。

不蹲，怎麼跳？
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在低潮但又一直逼自己「要振作」的朋友——蹲下來是為了下一次起跳，不蹲，怎麼跳
variations: 短版：人生不會永遠在低谷，但低谷的時候要先學會的不是爬出來，是坐下來
進階版：振作這兩個字，是別人對你的期待，不是你對自己的責任

en.content:
Park Shin-hye's line from Doctor Slump,
screenshot everywhere this year:

"Life won't stay in the valley forever.

But when you're in the valley,
the first thing to learn isn't how to climb out —

it's how to sit down."

When you're in a slump,
everyone tells you:
"Hang in there!"
"Think positive!"
"Other people have it worse!"

Nobody tells you —

you're allowed to just sit down.

You can finish that coffee you've been nursing for three hours.
You can rewatch that show for the fifth time.
You can do absolutely nothing all day
and stare at the ceiling.

Don't rush to "bounce back."

"Bounce back" is everyone else's expectation of you.
It's not your duty to yourself.

A valley isn't scary.

What's scary is —

feeling guilty
for even crouching down to rest.

You crouch down to launch the next jump.

Without crouching, how do you jump?
en.editorNote: Send to the friend in a slump who keeps forcing themselves to 'snap out of it' — you crouch down so you can launch the next jump. Without crouching, how do you jump?
variations: Short: life won't stay in the valley forever, but the first thing to learn there isn't how to climb out — it's how to sit down
Advanced: 'bounce back' is everyone else's expectation of you. It's not your duty to yourself.

items.sourceNote: 台劇《犀利人妻》「可是瑞凡，我們回不去了」經典台詞延伸，原創成年人關係回不去觀察

zh-tw.content:
《犀利人妻》那句已經被講爛的：

「可是瑞凡，
我們回不去了。」

二十歲看，覺得是分手台詞。
三十歲看，才發現——

它說的不只是愛情。

你跟最好的朋友冷戰半年，
後來想找他出來吃飯，
打字到一半，
你發現——

回不去了。

你離開那家公司之後，
同事傳訊息說「找一天聚一下」，
你笑著回「好啊」，
心裡知道——

回不去了。

你跟爸媽吵了那一架，
他們後來假裝沒發生，
你也假裝沒發生，
但每一通電話之間的空氣——

回不去了。

長大的痛點不是「失去」。

是「明明都還在，但已經不一樣了」。

他還在你的通訊錄，
但你不會打給他。

她還在你的限動觀眾名單，
但你們不會再見面。

那家店還開著，
但你不會再進去點那一杯。

回不去，
不代表不愛。

只是——

大家都長大了，
而長大這件事，
沒有回頭路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在想「為什麼我們再也回不到從前」的朋友——回不去不代表不愛，只是大家都長大了，而長大沒有回頭路
variations: 短版：可是瑞凡，我們回不去了
進階版：長大的痛點不是失去，是明明都還在，但已經不一樣了

en.content:
That line from The Fierce Wife,
quoted to death in Taiwan:

"But Rui-fan,
we can't go back."

At twenty, you hear it as a breakup line.
At thirty, you realize —

it's not just about love.

You and your best friend went six months without speaking.
You start typing to suggest dinner,
and halfway through, you realize —

you can't go back.

After you left that job,
a coworker texts "let's grab a meal sometime,"
you reply "sure!" with a smile,
knowing —

you can't go back.

You had that one fight with your parents.
They pretend it didn't happen.
You pretend it didn't happen.
But the air between every phone call after —

can't go back.

The ache of growing up isn't "losing."

It's "everything is still here, but it's not the same anymore."

He's still in your contacts,
but you won't call.

She's still on your close friends list,
but you won't meet again.

That café is still open,
but you won't go in and order that drink.

"Can't go back"
doesn't mean "didn't love."

It just means —

everyone grew up,
and growing up
has no return ticket.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still wondering 'why can't we ever go back to how we were' — can't go back doesn't mean didn't love. Everyone just grew up, and growing up has no return ticket.
variations: Short: but Rui-fan, we can't go back.
Advanced: the ache of growing up isn't losing. It's 'everything is still here, but it's not the same anymore.'

items.sourceNote: 2026治癒系韓劇「你的幸福為什麼要問別人」金句延伸，原創成年人決定權觀察

zh-tw.content:
2026年最被截圖的韓劇台詞：

「你的幸福，
為什麼要問別人？」

短短一句，
但我看完之後愣了三十秒。

因為我發現——

我買一件衣服，會問朋友好不好看。
我換一份工作，會問家人值不值得。
我喜歡一個人，會問身邊的人「他適合我嗎」。
我想去旅行，會問老闆能不能請假。

好像我這一輩子，
都在等別人簽核我的幸福。

別人說「好」，我才敢開心。
別人說「不好」，我立刻收回。

可是——

他們又不是你。

他們的人生，
他們自己都還沒搞清楚，
憑什麼幫你做決定？

從今天開始，
試著做一件事——

做完，再告訴他們。

不是請示，是通知。

你會發現，
當你不再問的時候，
他們也沒那麼想管了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個做任何決定都要先問遍全世界的朋友——你的幸福不需要別人簽核，做完再通知就好
variations: 短版：你的幸福，為什麼要問別人？
進階版：不是請示，是通知——當你不再問，他們也沒那麼想管了

en.content:
The most screenshotted K-drama line of 2026:

"Your happiness —
why are you asking other people about it?"

One short line,
and I sat stunned for thirty seconds.

Because I realized —

I ask friends if a shirt looks good before buying it.
I ask family if a new job is worth taking.
I ask people around me "is he right for me" before liking someone.
I ask my boss if I can travel.

My whole life,
I've been waiting for other people to approve my happiness.

They say "yes" — then I dare to be happy.
They say "no" — I immediately pull back.

But —

they're not you.

They haven't figured out their own lives yet.
What gives them the right to decide yours?

From today, try one thing —

Do it first. Tell them after.

Not asking permission. Just informing.

You'll find that
the moment you stop asking,
they stop caring quite so much.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who polls the entire world before making any decision — your happiness doesn't need anyone's approval. Just inform them after.
variations: Short: your happiness — why are you asking other people about it?
Advanced: not asking permission. Just informing. The moment you stop asking, they stop caring.

items.sourceNote: 2026台劇《動物園》職涯轉彎主題延伸，原創中年轉職的勇氣觀察

zh-tw.content:
《動物園》邵雨薇那場戲——
從廣告公司創意總監，
變成動物園的實習保育員。

她蹲在猩猩面前，
說了一句台詞：

「我以前以為自己很厲害，
是因為我能掌控一切。

現在我才知道——

真正的厲害，
是承認自己什麼都不會，
然後從零開始學。」

三十五歲轉職，
最難的不是技術。

是面子。

是你以前在會議室裡指點江山，
現在要跟二十二歲的同事說「請教一下」。

是你以前的職稱是「總監」，
現在的職稱是「實習」。

是你以前年薪兩百萬，
現在月薪三萬五。

但你會發現——

當你願意把「總監」這個包袱放下，
你才終於變回那個——

二十二歲、什麼都敢試、什麼都不怕的自己。

年紀大不可怕。

可怕的是——

用「我這個年紀」這四個字，
把自己關起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直想轉換跑道、但被「我這個年紀」綁住的朋友——把總監這個包袱放下，你才會變回什麼都敢試的自己
variations: 短版：真正的厲害，是承認自己什麼都不會，然後從零開始學
進階版：年紀大不可怕，可怕的是用「我這個年紀」把自己關起來

en.content:
There's a scene in Zoo —
Shao Yu-wei goes from creative director at an ad agency
to an intern keeper at the zoo.

She crouches in front of a gorilla
and delivers this line:

"I used to think I was impressive
because I could control everything.

Now I know —

real strength
is admitting you know nothing
and starting over from zero."

Switching careers at thirty-five.
The hardest part isn't the skills.

It's the ego.

It's that you used to run the room in meetings,
and now you're asking a twenty-two-year-old coworker "could you show me?"

Your old title was "Director."
Your new title is "Intern."

You used to make two hundred grand.
Now you make thirty-five hundred a month.

But here's what you find —

the moment you put down the weight of "Director,"
you finally become —

that twenty-two-year-old again. The one who tried everything. Was afraid of nothing.

Getting older isn't the scary part.

The scary part is —

using the words "at my age"
to lock yourself in a box.
en.editorNote: Send to a friend who keeps wanting to switch careers but stays trapped by 'at my age' — put down the Director title and you'll find the version of you who was afraid of nothing
variations: Short: real strength is admitting you know nothing and starting over from zero
Advanced: getting older isn't scary. The scary part is using 'at my age' to lock yourself in a box

items.sourceNote: 2026韓劇《依然閃亮》跨越十年初戀主題延伸，原創成年人重逢觀察

zh-tw.content:
《依然閃亮》朴珍榮跟金玟周
隔了十年再見面，
第一句話不是「你過得好嗎」，
是——

「你還是
喜歡靠窗的位置嗎？」

看到這裡，
我把手機放下，
深呼吸了一下。

因為這就是初戀最殘忍的地方——

你以為你忘了，
但你記得他不吃香菜。
你記得他過敏會打三個噴嚏，不是兩個。
你記得他笑的時候，
左邊酒窩比右邊深一點。

這些細節，
你沒有刻意記，
但它們一直都在那裡。

十年後重逢，
最尷尬的不是不熟悉，
是——

你太熟悉了。

熟悉到你怕一開口，
就洩漏這十年來，
你其實偷偷地，
還在用他的偏好過生活。

你之所以坐靠窗，
不是因為你喜歡。

是因為你習慣了，
身邊坐的是他。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個假裝自己早就忘了初戀、但每次點咖啡都還是「他喜歡的那種」的朋友——你以為你忘了，但身體記得
variations: 短版：你還是喜歡靠窗的位置嗎？
進階版：你之所以坐靠窗，不是因為你喜歡，是因為你習慣了身邊坐的是他

en.content:
In Still Bright,
Park Jin-young and Kim Min-ju
meet again after ten years apart.
His first line isn't "how have you been."

It's —

"Do you still
like the seat by the window?"

I put down my phone
and took a long breath.

Because this is what's brutal about first love —

you think you've forgotten,
but you remember he doesn't eat cilantro.
You remember his allergies make him sneeze three times, not two.
You remember when he smiles,
the left dimple is slightly deeper than the right.

You never tried to memorize these things.
They just stayed.

Reuniting ten years later,
the awkward part isn't being strangers.

It's —

being too familiar.

Familiar enough that you're scared the moment you speak,
you'll give away the secret —

that for ten years,
you've quietly
been living by his preferences.

The reason you sit by the window
isn't because you like it.

It's because you got used to
him sitting next to you.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who pretends they're long over their first love but still orders 'the kind he liked' every time — you think you forgot, but the body remembers
variations: Short: do you still like the seat by the window?
Advanced: the reason you sit by the window isn't because you like it. It's because you got used to him sitting next to you.

items.sourceNote: 2026 Netflix《莎拉的真偽人生》詐騙犯與真相主題延伸，原創社群時代偽裝觀察

zh-tw.content:
《莎拉的真偽人生》最戳人的一句：

「真相像光一樣，
讓人難以直視；

謊言就像美麗的晚霞——
為一切事物增色，
直到被戳破為止。」

我關掉電視，
滑了一下 IG。

然後愣住了。

因為我發現——

我們每一個人，
都正在過著一種「晚霞版本」的人生。

那張濾鏡過的咖啡照，
是晚霞。
那段剪掉前後三十秒爭吵的旅行 vlog，
是晚霞。
那則「最近真的很幸福」的限動，
是晚霞。

我們不是詐騙犯，
我們只是——

比較會選角度。

但問題是——

看久了別人的晚霞，
你會以為自己的白天
是壞掉的。

關掉手機，
抬頭看一下真正的天空。

那才是光。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每次滑 IG 都覺得自己人生很失敗的朋友——別人都在過晚霞，你過的才是真正的白天
variations: 短版：謊言就像美麗的晚霞，為一切事物增色，直到被戳破為止
進階版：我們不是詐騙犯，我們只是比較會選角度——但看久了別人的晚霞，你會以為自己的白天是壞掉的

en.content:
The line from The Art of Sarah that stops you cold:

"Truth is like light —
hard to look at directly.

Lies are like a beautiful sunset —
they color everything,
until someone pulls them apart."

I turned off the TV
and opened Instagram.

Then I just sat there.

Because I realized —

every single one of us
is living a sunset version of our lives.

The filtered coffee photo —
sunset.
The travel vlog with the thirty-second fight edited out —
sunset.
The story that says "genuinely happy lately" —
sunset.

We're not con artists.
We just —

pick better angles.

But here's the problem —

stare at other people's sunsets long enough,
and you start to think your own daylight
is broken.

Put down the phone.
Look up at the actual sky.

That's the light.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who scrolls Instagram and feels their life is a failure — everyone else is posting sunsets. You're the one living in actual daylight.
variations: Short: lies are like a beautiful sunset — they color everything, until someone pulls them apart
Advanced: we're not con artists, we just pick better angles — but stare at other people's sunsets long enough, and you start to think your own daylight is broken

items.sourceNote: 2026《莎拉的真偽人生》「人們相信的就是真相」主題延伸，原創信念與事實的觀察

zh-tw.content:
《莎拉的真偽人生》申惠善那句——

「人們往往認為，
自己相信的，
就是真相。」

短短一句，
但我想了很久。

因為我發現——

我們吵架，
從來不是為了「對錯」。

是為了——

「我相信的，比你相信的更接近真相。」

你媽說「我都是為你好」，
她真的相信。
你前任說「我從來沒有騙過你」，
他也真的相信。
你的同事說「我那天沒有講你壞話」，
他發誓他真的相信。

問題不是他們在說謊。

問題是——

人類有一種天賦，
叫做「把自己想做的事，
包裝成自己該做的事」。

然後——

再相信它一輩子。

所以下次有人說「我是真心的」，
你不用急著反駁。

他真的是。

只是「真心」這兩個字，
從來都沒有保證過——

他是對的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直被別人「真心話」綁架的朋友——真心不等於正確，他相信不代表那就是真相
variations: 短版：人們往往認為，自己相信的，就是真相
進階版：「真心」這兩個字，從來都沒有保證過他是對的

en.content:
Shin Hye-sun's line in The Art of Sarah —

"People tend to believe
that what they believe
is the truth."

One short line.
I sat with it for a long time.

Because I realized —

we never argue
about right and wrong.

We argue about —

"what I believe is closer to the truth than what you believe."

Your mom says "I'm doing this for your own good."
She genuinely believes it.
Your ex says "I never lied to you."
He genuinely believes it.
Your coworker swears "I didn't talk about you that day."
He genuinely believes it.

The problem isn't that they're lying.

The problem is —

humans have this gift
for wrapping "what I want to do"
in the package of "what I should do."

And then —

believing it for the rest of their lives.

So next time someone says "I mean it sincerely,"
you don't have to fight them.

They do mean it.

It's just that the word "sincere"
has never once guaranteed —

that they're right.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps getting steamrolled by other people's 'sincere intentions' — sincerity isn't accuracy. They believe it. That doesn't make it true.
variations: Short: people tend to believe that what they believe is the truth
Advanced: the word 'sincere' has never once guaranteed that they're right

items.sourceNote: 2026療癒韓劇「平凡人生」主題延伸，原創成年後的普通觀察

zh-tw.content:
最近被截圖最多的韓劇台詞：

「長大後光是維持普通的生活，
就已經耗盡全力。

承認自己
只想做個平凡人，
一點都不丟臉。」

我看完之後，
把手機放下，
眼眶有點熱。

因為小時候，
大人都跟我們說——

「你要成為很特別的人。」
「你不可以平凡。」
「平凡的人會被淹沒。」

所以我們拼了命想變特別。
考第一名、考好學校、進好公司、
年薪要有六位數、要在三十歲前買房、
要結婚、要生小孩、要孝順、
要在 IG 上看起來很豐盛。

然後三十五歲那天，
你坐在公司樓下抽菸，
突然發現——

我連準時下班，
都已經是奢侈。

那一刻你才明白——

「普通的生活」
從來都不是門檻，
它是成就。

能準時吃晚餐，能睡飽，
能週末出去走走，
能跟家人講話不會吵架。

這些，
才是真正困難的事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被「要成為很特別的人」綁架了二十年、現在累到只想好好過日子的朋友——平凡不是失敗，是成就
variations: 短版：承認自己只想做個平凡人，一點都不丟臉
進階版：「普通的生活」從來都不是門檻，它是成就

en.content:
The most screenshotted K-drama line lately:

"Just maintaining an ordinary life as an adult
drains everything you have.

Admitting
you just want to be an ordinary person
is not shameful at all."

I finished the scene,
put down my phone,
and my eyes got hot.

Because when we were kids,
adults all told us —

"Be someone special."
"Don't be ordinary."
"Ordinary people get swallowed up."

So we sprinted to become special.
Top of the class, good school, good company,
six-figure salary, house before thirty,
marriage, kids, filial,
look abundant on Instagram.

Then one day at thirty-five,
you're smoking outside the office
and it hits you —

leaving work on time
is already a luxury.

That's the moment you understand —

"an ordinary life"
was never the floor.
It's the ceiling.

Eating dinner on time. Sleeping enough.
Going somewhere on the weekend.
Talking to your family without fighting.

These are the things
that are actually hard.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's been held hostage by 'be someone special' for twenty years and now just wants to live a quiet life — ordinary isn't failure. It's the achievement.
variations: Short: admitting you just want to be ordinary is not shameful at all
Advanced: an ordinary life was never the floor. It's the ceiling.

items.sourceNote: 2026《淚之女王》愛情與承諾主題延伸，原創成年人的告白觀察

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》金秀賢對金智媛說的：

「我不會說
『我一輩子都會愛你』
這種話。

我只會說——

今天，
我選擇你。

明天醒來，
我會再選一次。」

小時候看偶像劇，
最喜歡聽「我會愛你一輩子」。

長大以後，
你才知道——

「一輩子」太輕了。

輕到任何人喝醉、心動、寂寞，
都能在三秒鐘內說出口。

真正重的，
是「每天」。

是你今天累得要死，
還是選擇打那通電話。
是你今天明明很煩，
還是選擇聽他講完那件無聊的小事。
是你今天明明可以離開，
還是選擇留下來。

愛情從來不是一張承諾書。

是——

每天早上醒來，
你都還想再選他一次。

選不下去的那天，
才是真正的結束。

不是吵架，不是出軌，
是——

「今天我不想選了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在等對方說「一輩子」的朋友——一輩子太便宜，「每天都還想選你一次」才是真的
variations: 短版：我不會說一輩子都愛你，我只會說——今天，我選擇你
進階版：愛情從來不是承諾書，是每天早上醒來，你都還想再選他一次

en.content:
What Kim Soo-hyun says to Kim Ji-won in Queen of Tears:

"I won't say
'I'll love you forever.'

I'll only say —

Today,
I choose you.

Tomorrow when I wake up,
I'll choose you again."

When we were young watching dramas,
the favorite line was always "I'll love you forever."

Then you grew up
and realized —

"forever" is light.

Light enough that anyone — drunk, infatuated, lonely —
can say it in three seconds.

The thing that's actually heavy
is "today."

Today you're exhausted,
and you still chose to make the call.
Today you're in a terrible mood,
and you still chose to listen to his boring little story.
Today you could leave,
and you still chose to stay.

Love was never a contract.

It's —

waking up every morning
and still wanting to choose him again.

The day you can't choose anymore
is the real ending.

Not the fight. Not the affair.
Just —

"Today, I don't want to choose."
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still waiting for someone to say 'forever' — forever is cheap. 'I want to choose you again today' is the real one.
variations: Short: I won't say I'll love you forever. I'll only say — today, I choose you
Advanced: love was never a contract. It's waking up every morning and still wanting to choose him again

items.sourceNote: 2026《低谷醫生》朴信惠朴炯植躺平休息哲學延伸，原創成年人疲憊觀察

zh-tw.content:
《低谷醫生》最被截圖的那句：

「既然都倒下了，
就躺著吧。

不一定要立刻爬起來，
不一定要馬上想通，

地板很涼，
躺一下沒關係。」

看到這句的當下，
我整個人愣了三秒。

因為從小到大，
沒有人這樣對我說過。

他們都說——

「跌倒了就要趕快站起來。」
「你要更努力。」
「別人都做得到，為什麼你不行？」

好像「倒下」是一種羞恥，
「休息」是一種逃避，
「不想再撐」是一種失敗。

可是長大之後你才明白——

有些倒下，
不是因為你不夠強，
是因為你撐太久了。

那種累，
不是睡一覺就會好的。

它需要你——

真的躺下來，
不滑手機、不回訊息、
不思考下一步要怎麼辦，

就只是
讓自己
靜止一下。

地板真的很涼。

而且——

它從來不會催你起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個倒下了還在硬撐、覺得「休息=罪惡」的朋友——地板很涼，躺一下沒關係，它不會催你起來
variations: 短版：既然都倒下了，就躺著吧——不一定要立刻爬起來
進階版：有些倒下不是因為你不夠強，是因為你撐太久了

en.content:
The most screenshotted line from Doctor Slump:

"Since you've already fallen down,
just lie there.

You don't have to get up right away.
You don't have to figure it out right now.

The floor is cool.
It's okay to lie there for a while."

I saw that line and froze for three seconds.

Because growing up,
no one ever said this to me.

They all said —

"If you fall, get up fast."
"Work harder."
"Everyone else can do it. Why can't you?"

As if falling down was shameful,
resting was running away,
"I can't do this anymore" was failure.

But as an adult you finally understand —

some falls
aren't because you weren't strong enough.
They're because you held it together for too long.

That kind of exhaustion
doesn't get fixed by one good sleep.

It needs you to —

actually lie down.
No phone. No replying to messages.
No planning what to do next.

Just
let yourself
be still.

The floor really is cool.

And —

it never rushes you to stand up.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who fell down and is still pushing through, who treats 'resting' like a sin — the floor is cool. It's okay. It won't rush you.
variations: Short: since you've already fallen down, just lie there — you don't have to get up right away
Advanced: some falls aren't because you weren't strong enough. They're because you held it together for too long.

items.sourceNote: 2026《愛情怎麼翻譯？》金宣虎高允貞溫柔終止主題延伸，原創親密關係觀察

zh-tw.content:
《愛情怎麼翻譯？》金宣虎那句話，
我看了三遍：

「溫柔，
是很可怕的東西。

一想到
有一天它會終止——

它就變成
世界上最可怕的東西。」

以前不懂這句話。

後來談過幾次戀愛之後，
才終於明白——

讓人崩潰的，
從來不是「你對我不好」。

是「你曾經對我這麼好」。

他以前會記得你不吃香菜，
會在你下班那條路上等你，
會聽你講你媽又煩了你什麼事。

後來他不記得了。
後來他不等了。
後來他在你講話到一半，
低頭滑手機。

你不是失去了一個人，
你是失去了——

那個曾經被他細心對待的自己。

所以溫柔最可怕的地方在於——

它讓你知道，
原來自己也可以被好好對待。

然後有一天
它消失了，
你就再也回不去
那個「不知道好是什麼樣子」的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個分手後一直走不出來、不知道自己在難過什麼的朋友——你不是失去一個人，你是失去那個被他好好對待的自己
variations: 短版：溫柔很可怕，因為一想到它會終止，就變成世界上最可怕的東西
進階版：你不是失去了一個人，你是失去了那個曾經被他細心對待的自己

en.content:
Kim Seon-ho's line in Can This Love Be Translated?
I watched it three times:

"Tenderness
is a terrifying thing.

The moment you realize
one day it will end —

it becomes
the most terrifying thing in the world."

I didn't understand this line at first.

Then after a few relationships,
I finally got it —

what breaks you
was never "you stopped being good to me."

It's "you used to be this good to me."

He used to remember you don't eat cilantro.
He used to wait at the corner near your office.
He used to listen to you complain about your mom.

Then he didn't remember.
Then he didn't wait.
Then he looked down at his phone
while you were mid-sentence.

You didn't lose a person.
You lost —

the version of yourself
that was once carefully looked after.

That's the scariest part of tenderness —

it shows you
that you can actually be treated well.

And one day
when it disappears,
you can't go back
to the version of you that didn't know what "good" felt like.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who can't get over the breakup and doesn't know what they're really mourning — you didn't lose a person. You lost the version of you that was once carefully looked after.
variations: Short: tenderness is terrifying because the moment you realize it will end, it becomes the most terrifying thing
Advanced: you didn't lose a person. You lost the version of yourself that was once carefully looked after.

items.sourceUrl: https://emilywei1027.com/雖然是精神病但沒關係
items.sourceNote: 經典韓劇《雖然是精神病但沒關係》信任主題2026重新流行，原創人際關係觀察
tags: 雖然是精神病但沒關係

zh-tw.content:
《雖然是精神病但沒關係》最近又被翻出來瘋傳的這句：

「真正的壞人，
不是會傷害你的人。

是——

不管你說什麼
他都不相信的人。」

第一次看的時候沒有感覺。

直到上週
我跟我媽吵了一架，
才終於懂了。

我說：
「媽，我最近真的壓力很大。」
她回：「年輕人能有什麼壓力。」

我說：
「我不是不想結婚，我是還沒準備好。」
她回：「你就是太挑了。」

我說：
「我喜歡我現在的工作。」
她回：「不要騙自己。」

那一刻我才發現——

傷害我最深的，
從來不是罵我的人。

是
從來不相信我的人。

是
我把心剖開來給他看，
他卻說
「你不是真的這樣想。」

那是一種——

比被打罵更深的孤單。

因為連你自己
說出來的話，
都已經
不被允許
是真的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直被家人否定情緒、覺得自己連感受都要先被審核的朋友——真正的傷害不是被罵，是說什麼都不被相信
variations: 短版：真正的壞人不是傷害你的人，是不管你說什麼都不相信你的人
進階版：最深的孤單不是被罵，是你說出來的話連自己都不被允許是真的

en.content:
The line from It's Okay to Not Be Okay that resurfaced lately:

"The truly bad person
is not the one who hurts you.

It's —

the one who doesn't believe you
no matter what you say."

First time I watched it, I felt nothing.

Then last week
I got into a fight with my mom
and suddenly I understood.

I said:
"Mom, I've been really stressed."
She said: "Young people don't have real stress."

I said:
"It's not that I don't want to get married. I'm just not ready."
She said: "You're just too picky."

I said:
"I actually like my job."
She said: "Don't lie to yourself."

That's when it hit me —

the deepest hurt
was never from the people who yelled at me.

It was
the people who refused to believe me.

The ones I opened my chest for
and they said
"that's not what you actually think."

That's —

a lonelier kind of loneliness
than being insulted.

Because even the words
you speak yourself
are no longer allowed
to be real.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose feelings get denied by family, who has to get their emotions pre-approved before they're allowed to feel them — the worst hurt isn't being yelled at. It's not being believed.
variations: Short: the truly bad person isn't the one who hurts you. It's the one who doesn't believe you no matter what you say.
Advanced: the deepest loneliness is when even the words you speak yourself aren't allowed to be real

items.sourceNote: 2026療癒系韓劇「家有人等」主題延伸，原創成年遊子的回家觀察

zh-tw.content:
2026 年最被傳閱的韓劇台詞之一：

「不管在外面
受了多少委屈，

只要想到
家裡還有人
等著你、愛著你，

就好像
又有力氣
面對明天了。」

我看完那一集，
打開冰箱，
看到我媽前天寄來的滷牛腱還沒吃完，

突然就哭了。

不是因為感動，
是因為——

我已經忘記，
上一次
「有人等我」的感覺
是什麼時候了。

租屋處沒有人。
手機沒有人。
下班路上沒有人。

你會慢慢習慣這種空，
習慣到
以為這就是正常。

直到那一塊牛腱
從冰箱裡看著你，

你才想起來——

原來「被等」這件事，
從來不是浪漫劇情，

是
大人世界裡，
最稀有的
生活必需品。

打電話回家。

不一定要講重要的事。

讓他們知道——

你也記得
他們在等。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一個人在外地、忘記家是什麼感覺的朋友——被等不是浪漫劇情，是成年人最稀有的生活必需品
variations: 短版：只要想到家裡還有人等著你，就好像又有力氣面對明天了
進階版：「被等」從來不是浪漫劇情，是大人世界裡最稀有的生活必需品

en.content:
One of the most-shared K-drama lines of 2026:

"No matter how much
the world hurts you out there,

the moment you remember
someone at home
is waiting for you, loving you,

it's like
you've got just enough strength
to face tomorrow again."

I finished that episode,
opened the fridge,
saw the braised beef shank my mom mailed me two days ago, still half-eaten,

and suddenly I was crying.

Not because I was moved.
Because —

I couldn't remember
the last time
the feeling of "someone is waiting for me"
was real.

No one in the apartment.
No one on the phone.
No one on the walk home.

You slowly get used to that emptiness.
Used to it enough
that you think it's just normal.

Until that piece of beef shank
looks back at you from the fridge,

and you remember —

being waited for
was never a romantic plot device.

It's
the rarest necessity
of adult life.

Call home.

You don't have to talk about anything important.

Just let them know —

you remember
they're waiting too.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend living alone in another city who's forgotten what 'home' feels like — being waited for isn't a romance trope. It's the rarest necessity of adult life.
variations: Short: the moment you remember someone at home is waiting for you, you have just enough strength to face tomorrow
Advanced: being waited for was never a romantic plot device. It's the rarest necessity of adult life.

items.sourceNote: 2026《莎拉的真偽人生》Netflix 春節爆紅，真相與謊言主題延伸，原創社群時代觀察

zh-tw.content:
《莎拉的真偽人生》那句被截圖瘋傳：

「真相像光一樣，
讓人難以直視；

謊言就像
美麗的晚霞，
為一切事物增色，

直到
被戳破為止。」

看到這句話，
我默默打開 Instagram。

滑了三分鐘。

看到那個朋友
上禮拜還在抱怨工作，
今天就 po 出咖啡廳精修照，
配文寫「感謝這份熱愛」。

看到那對情侶
上個月才剛吵到分房睡，
這個月就在峇里島合照，
配文寫「最好的我們」。

看到自己
昨天加班到崩潰，
今天還是 po 了一張陽光從窗戶灑進辦公室的照片，
配文寫「努力的人運氣不會太差」。

那一刻我突然懂了——

我們都不是在說謊。

我們只是
選了晚霞那一面
給別人看。

因為光的那一面
太刺眼了，
連自己都不敢看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一邊在 IG 假裝過得很好、一邊半夜偷哭的朋友——我們都不是在說謊，只是選了晚霞那一面給別人看
variations: 短版：真相像光一樣難以直視，謊言像晚霞美得讓人不想戳破
進階版：我們不是在說謊，只是選了晚霞那一面給別人看，因為光的那一面太刺眼了

en.content:
The screenshotted line from The Real Sara:

"Truth is like light —
hard to look at directly.

Lies are like
a beautiful sunset.
They color everything,

until
someone calls them out."

I read that line
and quietly opened Instagram.

Scrolled for three minutes.

Saw that friend
who was venting about work last week
post a perfectly edited cafe photo today,
caption: "grateful for this passion."

Saw that couple
who were sleeping in separate rooms last month
post a Bali couple photo this month,
caption: "the best version of us."

Saw myself
staying late and breaking down yesterday,
still posting a sunbeam-through-office-window shot today,
caption: "good things happen to people who try."

That's when it hit me —

none of us are lying.

We're just
showing people
the sunset side.

Because the light side
is too harsh.
Even we can't look at it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend faking it on IG by day, crying at 2am by night — none of us are lying. We're just showing people the sunset side because the light side is too harsh to look at.
variations: Short: truth is like light, too harsh to look at. Lies are like sunsets, too pretty to call out.
Advanced: we're not lying. We're just showing people the sunset side, because the light side is too harsh — even for ourselves.

items.sourceNote: 2026台劇《影后》Netflix 主題延伸，「我有沒有電」自我覺察金句，原創都市疲憊觀察

zh-tw.content:
《影后》最後一集那句，
我被釘在沙發上五分鐘：

「我從來沒問過自己
還有沒有電。」

我才發現——

我會問手機有沒有電。
會問車子油還夠不夠。
會問另一半今天累不累。
會問同事這禮拜還撐得住嗎。

但我從來不問
自己
還剩多少電。

我醒著就工作。
累了就刷手機。
撐不住就喝咖啡。
崩潰就告訴自己
「再撐一下，週末就到了」。

週末到了。

我躺在床上滑手機，
累得連睡都睡不著，
然後星期一又再來一次。

我活得像
一台從來沒被插過電的手機，
靠著每天 5% 的剩餘電量
在運轉。

而我從來
沒有問過自己
還剩多少。

今晚——

請你
認真問一次自己：

「你還有電嗎？」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直幫別人充電、卻從來沒檢查過自己電量的朋友——你問過手機、問過車子、問過所有人，今晚請認真問一次自己
variations: 短版：我從來沒問過自己還有沒有電
進階版：我活得像一台從來沒被插過電的手機，靠著每天 5% 的剩餘電量在運轉

en.content:
That line from the final episode of The Outstanding Actress
pinned me to the couch for five minutes:

"I've never once asked myself
if I still have battery left."

That's when I realized —

I ask if my phone is charged.
I ask if there's enough gas in the car.
I ask my partner if they're tired.
I ask coworkers if they can make it through the week.

But I never ask
myself
how much I have left.

I work when I'm awake.
I scroll when I'm tired.
I drink coffee when I'm collapsing.
I tell myself "hold on, the weekend's almost here"
when I'm breaking.

The weekend comes.

I lie in bed scrolling,
too exhausted to even sleep,
and then Monday starts again.

I'm living like
a phone that's never been plugged in,
running on the 5% it had left this morning.

And I've never
once asked
how much is left.

Tonight —

please
actually ask yourself:

"Do you still have battery?"
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who charges everyone else but never checks their own battery — you've asked your phone, your car, everyone. Tonight, actually ask yourself.
variations: Short: I've never once asked myself if I still have battery left
Advanced: I'm living like a phone that's never been plugged in, running on the 5% it had this morning.

items.sourceNote: Hometown Cha Cha Cha 洪斗植經典金句 2026 重新流行，原創成年人面對生活的觀察

zh-tw.content:
最近又被翻出來的洪斗植金句：

「就算撐傘，
還是會被淋濕。

那就
把手張開，
迎接這場雨。」

二十幾歲看這句，
覺得太浪漫。

三十幾歲再看，
才終於懂——

他不是在說雨。

他是在說
你已經反覆證明過的那件事：

再小心，
還是會被傷害。
再努力，
還是會被誤解。
再防備，
還是會碰到那個對你不好的人。

所以——

與其每天
抓著那把破洞的傘
還假裝沒事，

不如
承認自己會被淋濕。

然後
張開手。

讓雨
下完。

你會發現——

你以為會把你打濕到死的東西，
其實也只是
一場雨而已。

會停的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個拚命撐傘還是被人生淋成落湯雞的朋友——你不是不夠小心，是雨太大。張開手，讓它下完，它會停
variations: 短版：就算撐傘還是會被淋濕，那就把手張開迎接這場雨
進階版：你以為會把你打濕到死的東西，其實也只是一場雨而已——會停的

en.content:
Hong Doo-shik's line from Hometown Cha-Cha-Cha resurfacing again:

"Even if you carry an umbrella,
you'll still get wet.

So just
open your hands
and welcome the rain."

In your twenties, it sounds too romantic.

In your thirties, you finally get it —

he's not talking about rain.

He's talking about
the thing you've already proven, over and over:

No matter how careful you are,
you still get hurt.
No matter how hard you try,
you still get misunderstood.
No matter how guarded you are,
you still meet the person who treats you badly.

So —

instead of
clutching a broken umbrella
and pretending you're dry,

just
admit that you're going to get wet.

Then
open your hands.

Let the rain
finish.

You'll realize —

the thing you thought would soak you forever
was, in the end,
just a rainstorm.

It stops.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend white-knuckling a broken umbrella through life's downpour — you're not careless. It's just heavy rain. Open your hands. It stops.
variations: Short: even with an umbrella you still get wet. Open your hands and welcome the rain.
Advanced: the thing you thought would soak you forever was, in the end, just a rainstorm. It stops.

items.sourceNote: 《今生也請多指教》成年裝懂主題 2026 重新流行，原創成年人裝大人觀察

zh-tw.content:
《今生也請多指教》那句
最近又被瘋狂截圖：

「人到死
都不會懂事。

大家
都是
裝出來的。」

我看到這句話，
第一個反應是想哭。

因為我以為——

三十歲就會變成大人。
結果三十歲那天
我還在跟我媽吵
為什麼她又把我的東西收掉。

三十五歲就會懂愛情。
結果三十五歲了
我還是會因為一則「在嗎」
失眠到天亮。

四十歲就會懂工作。
結果四十歲了
我還是會
在會議結束之後
躲到廁所深呼吸三次
才敢面對下一場。

原來——

所有看起來很「大人」的人，
都跟你一樣
邊裝邊學、邊學邊崩潰。

他們只是
比較會藏。

所以下次
你又覺得自己
「怎麼還沒長大」的時候——

請記得：

沒有人
真的長大了。

大家都
在裝。

你已經
做得很好了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直覺得自己「怎麼還沒變成大人」的朋友——沒有人真的長大，大家都在裝。你已經做得很好了
variations: 短版：人到死都不會懂事，大家都是裝出來的
進階版：所有看起來很「大人」的人，都跟你一樣邊裝邊學、邊學邊崩潰，他們只是比較會藏

en.content:
The line from Doctors that's getting screenshotted again:

"No one
figures it out
before they die.

Everyone
is just
pretending."

I read it
and my first reaction was to cry.

Because I thought —

at 30, I'd become an adult.
On my 30th birthday
I was still fighting with my mom
about why she moved my stuff again.

At 35, I'd understand love.
At 35, I still lost sleep
over a single "u up?" text.

At 40, I'd understand work.
At 40, I still
hide in the bathroom after meetings
taking three deep breaths
before I can face the next one.

Turns out —

every person who looks like a real adult
is doing the same thing as you:
faking it, learning it, falling apart in between.

They just
hide it better.

So next time
you catch yourself wondering
"why haven't I grown up yet" —

please remember:

no one
has actually grown up.

Everyone is
pretending.

You're already
doing great.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps wondering when they'll finally feel like a real adult — no one has actually grown up. Everyone is pretending. You're already doing great.
variations: Short: no one figures it out before they die. Everyone is just pretending.
Advanced: every adult you admire is faking it, learning it, falling apart in between — they just hide it better

items.sourceNote: 《苦盡柑來遇見你》IU 經典金句 2026 持續發酵，原創成年人撐不下去的觀察

zh-tw.content:
《苦盡柑來遇見你》那句
最近又被反覆截圖：

「總有一天
生活會難到
你以為自己撐不下去。

那時候——

不要躺著不動。

用盡力氣
掙扎。

告訴自己
不會死，
一定要活下去。

你會
再呼吸到的。」

以前看這句，
只覺得很文藝。

直到
那一天真的來了——

你才終於懂：

那不是文藝。
那是
一個經歷過的人
回頭來告訴你——

那種你以為過不去的東西，

會
過去。

只要
你還在
用力
掙扎。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正在低谷裡撐著的朋友——不要躺著不動，用盡力氣掙扎。你會再呼吸到的
variations: 短版：總有一天生活會難到你以為自己撐不下去——那時候不要躺著不動，用盡力氣掙扎
進階版：那不是文藝，那是一個經歷過的人回頭告訴你——你以為過不去的，會過去

en.content:
The line from When Life Gives You Tangerines
that keeps getting screenshotted:

"One day
life will get so hard
you'll think you can't go on.

When that happens —

don't just lie there.

Struggle
with everything you have.

Tell yourself
you won't die.
You have to live.

You'll be able
to breathe again."

The first time you read it,
it sounds poetic.

Until
that day actually comes —

then you finally get it:

it's not poetry.
it's
someone who's been there
turning around to tell you —

that thing you think you can't survive,

will
pass.

As long as
you keep
struggling.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend white-knuckling their way through a hard season — don't just lie there. Struggle with everything. You'll breathe again.
variations: Short: one day life gets so hard you think you can't go on — when that happens, don't lie still. Struggle.
Advanced: it's not poetry. It's someone who's been there telling you — what you can't survive, you will.

items.sourceNote: 2026 治癒系韓劇金句精選，原創普通人生主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
2026 最近被瘋傳的一句韓劇台詞：

「長大之後
光是
維持一個普通的生活，
就已經
耗盡全部力氣了。

承認自己
只想做個平凡人——

一點都
不丟臉。」

小時候
以為長大就是
變成厲害的人。

後來才知道——

長大
是
光把自己餵飽、
把房租繳掉、
把家人照顧好、
還能笑著回訊息，

就已經是
那一週
最了不起的成就。

你不是
沒有夢想了。

你是
把活著本身
當成夢想
在實現。

那已經
很厲害了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得自己「過得好平凡」而自責的朋友——光是把日子過好就已經耗盡全力，承認這件事，一點都不丟臉
variations: 短版：長大之後，光是維持一個普通的生活就已經耗盡全力——承認這件事，一點都不丟臉
進階版：你不是沒有夢想了，你是把活著本身當成夢想在實現

en.content:
The K-drama line going viral right now:

"After you grow up,
just
maintaining
an ordinary life
takes
everything you have.

Admitting
you only want to be ordinary —

is nothing
to be ashamed of."

As a kid
you thought growing up
meant becoming someone impressive.

Later you learn —

growing up
is
feeding yourself,
paying the rent,
showing up for your family,
and still answering texts with a smile,

and that
is already
the biggest accomplishment
of your week.

You haven't
stopped dreaming.

You're just
treating staying alive
as the dream —

and making it happen.

That's already
amazing.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps apologizing for having an 'ordinary' life — just keeping things together takes everything. There's nothing shameful about admitting that.
variations: Short: just maintaining an ordinary life takes everything — admitting that is nothing to be ashamed of
Advanced: you haven't stopped dreaming. You're treating staying alive as the dream — and making it happen

items.sourceNote: 《淚之女王》金智媛金秀賢經典台詞 2026 重新流行，原創婚姻長久觀察

zh-tw.content:
《淚之女王》最近又被翻出來的那句：

「我以為
婚姻
是兩個人變成一個人。

後來才發現——

是兩個人
一起學會
怎麼當
兩個人。」

年輕的時候
談戀愛
總希望對方什麼都懂你、
什麼都跟你一樣。

後來才知道——

真正能走得遠的關係
不是
「我們完全一樣」，

而是——

你有你的怪，
我有我的怪，

但我們
願意
把對方的怪
當成這個家
獨有的風景。

婚姻不是
融合。

是
兩個完整的人
站在一起——

還願意
每天早上
再選一次
彼此。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那對結婚很多年但還是在學習怎麼相處的夫妻——婚姻不是融合，是兩個完整的人每天早上再選一次彼此
variations: 短版：婚姻不是兩個人變成一個人，是兩個人一起學會怎麼當兩個人
進階版：真正能走得遠的關係，是把對方的怪當成這個家獨有的風景

en.content:
The Queen of Tears line resurfacing again:

"I thought
marriage
was two people becoming one.

Later I learned —

it's two people
learning together
how to be
two people."

When you're young
you date hoping
the other person understands everything,
matches everything.

Later you learn —

the relationships that go the distance
aren't
"we're exactly the same."

They're —

you have your weirdness,
I have my weirdness,

and we're
willing
to treat each other's weirdness
as the unique scenery
of this home.

Marriage isn't
merging.

It's
two whole people
standing side by side —

still choosing
each other
every morning,
again.
en.editorNote: Send to the long-married couple still figuring out how to live together — marriage isn't merging. It's two whole people choosing each other again every morning.
variations: Short: marriage isn't two people becoming one. It's two people learning how to be two people, together.
Advanced: the relationships that last treat each other's weirdness as the unique scenery of the home

items.sourceNote: 《莎拉的真偽人生》申惠善經典金句 2026 病毒式傳播，原創相信與真相主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
《莎拉的真偽人生》最近
被截圖瘋傳的一句：

「人們
往往
把自己相信的東西，
當成
真相。」

第一次看到的時候
沒什麼感覺。

後來
才發現
它在說的是
你身邊每一場
吵不完的架——

父母
相信他們是為你好，
所以他們做的事就是
「真相」。

前任
相信你變了，
所以你說再多
都不是
「真相」。

網路上的陌生人
相信你應該長那樣，
所以你呈現的真實就被當成
「人設」。

原來——

讓人最痛的
從來不是真相。

是
別人不願意
動搖
他們相信的版本，

所以
那個版本
就成了
你逃不掉的世界。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直在解釋自己卻沒人想聽的朋友——讓人最痛的從來不是真相，是別人不願意動搖他們相信的版本
variations: 短版：人們往往把自己相信的東西，當成真相
進階版：讓人最痛的從來不是真相，是別人不願意動搖他們相信的版本

en.content:
The line from The Real Has Come
that keeps getting screenshotted:

"People
often
treat what they believe
as
the truth."

The first time you read it
it doesn't land.

Later
you realize
it's describing
every unwinnable argument
in your life —

your parents
believe they're acting out of love,
so whatever they do
becomes
"the truth."

Your ex
believes you changed,
so nothing you say
ever becomes
"the truth."

Strangers online
believe you should look a certain way,
so what you actually are
gets dismissed as
"performance."

Turns out —

what hurts most
is never the truth.

It's
that other people
won't
let go
of the version they believe,

and so
that version
becomes
the world you can't escape.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps explaining themselves to people who won't listen — what hurts isn't the truth. It's people refusing to let go of the version they already believe.
variations: Short: people often treat what they believe as the truth
Advanced: what hurts most isn't the truth. It's other people refusing to let go of the version they believe

items.sourceNote: 台劇《影后》壁虎金句「手機不能沒電」2026 重新被翻紅，原創自我耗盡主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
台劇《影后》裡
那句又被翻出來的台詞：

「我的手機
絕對
不可以沒電——

可是我
從來沒問過自己

還有沒有電。」

你每天起床
第一件事
是把手機充飽。

睡前最後一件事
是看一眼
剩多少%。

上班路上忘了帶充電線
會回家拿。

可是——

你最近一次
問自己
「我今天還有沒有力氣」
是什麼時候？

你照顧手機
比照顧自己
還勤。

難怪你
總是
一回家
就直接黑屏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總說自己「再撐一下就好」的朋友——你照顧手機比照顧自己還勤，難怪你回家就直接黑屏
variations: 短版：手機不能沒電，但你從來沒問過自己還有沒有電
進階版：你照顧手機比照顧自己還勤，難怪你回家就直接黑屏

en.content:
That Born for the Spotlight line
that keeps resurfacing:

"My phone
absolutely
cannot run out of battery —

but I
have never asked myself

if I still have any."

The first thing you do
in the morning
is plug your phone in.

The last thing you check
before sleeping
is the battery percentage.

If you forget your charger
on the way to work,
you turn back to get it.

But —

when was the last time
you asked yourself,
"Do I still have anything left today?"

You take better care
of your phone
than of yourself.

No wonder
you get home
and the screen just goes black.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps saying 'just a little longer' — you maintain your phone better than yourself. No wonder you go dark the second you walk through the door.
variations: Short: your phone can't run out of battery, but you've never asked if you still have any
Advanced: you take better care of your phone than yourself — no wonder you go black-screen the moment you get home

items.sourceNote: 台劇《影后》「等你是個咖才有資格掉眼淚」金句 2026 再度爆紅，原創職場無聲處境改寫

zh-tw.content:
《影后》那句
讓人愣住的台詞：

「你聽好——

等你是個咖，
才有資格
掉眼淚。」

剛入行的時候
你以為
委屈
是會被看見的。

後來才發現——

你哭，
沒人在意。
你說累，
會被當成不能扛。
你說受傷，
別人會說「大家都這樣過來的」。

等你終於
做到那個位子，
你才發現——

不是你的眼淚
變得更有重量，

是
那個時候
才終於
有人
願意
停下來
聽你說話。

殘忍的不是
眼淚要等資格，

是
小時候沒人告訴你
這個世界
是這樣運作的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在底層默默承受的朋友——不是你的眼淚不夠重，是還沒到那個位子。別怪自己撐得太用力。
variations: 短版：等你是個咖，才有資格掉眼淚
進階版：殘忍的不是眼淚要等資格，是小時候沒人告訴你世界是這樣運作的

en.content:
The Born for the Spotlight line
that stops you cold:

"Listen carefully —

only when you're someone
do you get to cry."

When you first started out
you thought
your hurt
would be noticed.

Later you learned —

you cry, no one cares.
you say you're tired, they call you weak.
you say you're hurt, they say "we all went through it."

When you finally
make it to that seat,
you realize —

it's not that your tears
suddenly carry more weight,

it's just
that now
someone
is finally
willing
to stop
and listen.

The cruel part isn't
that tears require a rank.

It's that
no one told you
as a kid
this is how the world actually works.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still grinding silently at the bottom — it's not that your tears aren't heavy enough. You just haven't reached the seat where people stop and listen. Don't blame yourself for surviving too hard.
variations: Short: only when you're someone do you get to cry
Advanced: the cruel part isn't that tears require a rank. It's that no one told you as a kid this is how the world works.

items.sourceNote: 韓劇《今生也請多指教》「人到死都不會懂事，大家都是裝出來的」金句 2026 再流行，原創成熟假象主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
《今生也請多指教》
那句最近又被截圖的台詞：

「人到死
都不會懂事——

大家
都是
裝出來的。」

小時候
你看著大人
以為他們什麼都知道。

後來
你長大了，
才發現——

沒有人
突然某一天
就「成熟」了。

他們只是
比你早一點
學會
怎麼
在崩潰的時候
依然把咖啡喝完，

怎麼
在心裡哭的時候
依然開完那場會，

怎麼
在很想說「我撐不住」的時候
還是回一句
「沒事，我可以。」

所謂
大人——

不是
變堅強了，

是
變得
會裝了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總覺得別人都比自己「成熟」的朋友——沒有人真的成熟，大家只是比較早學會怎麼裝。你不是還沒長大，是還沒學會偽裝而已。
variations: 短版：人到死都不會懂事，大家都是裝出來的
進階版：所謂大人，不是變堅強了，是變得會裝了

en.content:
The line from See You in My 19th Life
that keeps getting screenshotted again:

"People
never actually grow up —

everyone
is just
faking it."

As a kid
you looked at adults
and thought they had it figured out.

Later
you grew up
and realized —

no one
woke up one morning
and suddenly became "mature."

They just learned
a little earlier than you

how to
finish the coffee
while breaking down,

how to
get through the meeting
while crying inside,

how to
answer "I'm fine, I've got this"
when what they meant was
"I can't do this anymore."

Being an adult
isn't
becoming stronger.

It's
becoming
better at pretending.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who thinks everyone else has their life together — no one is actually mature. They just learned to fake it sooner. You're not behind. You just haven't picked up the mask yet.
variations: Short: nobody actually grows up. Everyone is just faking it.
Advanced: being an adult isn't becoming stronger. It's becoming better at pretending.

items.sourceNote: 2026 治癒系韓劇金句「你的幸福為什麼要問別人」病毒式流行，原創自我決定權主題改寫

zh-tw.content:
2026 治癒系韓劇
最近被瘋傳的一句：

「你的幸福，

為什麼
要問
別人？」

你交往，要問家人有沒有意見。
你辭職，要問同事覺得會不會太可惜。
你搬出去住，要問鄰居會不會閒言閒語。
你結婚對象的條件，要問長輩通不通過。

你最後
甚至——

要問
從來沒在你人生裡
出現過的網友
你的選擇
夠不夠好。

結果——

你的人生
變成
所有人
都有發言權，

只有你自己
沒有。

別人
不會替你過這一輩子，

但你卻
讓他們
決定了
你怎麼過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個做每個決定都要問十個人意見的朋友——別人不會替你過這一輩子，但你卻讓他們決定了你怎麼過
variations: 短版：你的幸福，為什麼要問別人？
進階版：你的人生變成所有人都有發言權，只有你自己沒有

en.content:
The healing K-drama line
going viral right now:

"Your happiness —

why
are you asking
someone else?"

You date — you ask family if they approve.
You quit — you ask coworkers if it's a waste.
You move out — you ask neighbors if they'll gossip.
You choose a partner — you ask elders if they pass the test.

Eventually
you even —

ask strangers online
who have never appeared
in your actual life
whether your choice
is good enough.

And then —

your life
becomes
a room where everyone
has a vote,

except
you.

None of them
will live this life for you,

but you let them
decide
how you live it.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who consults ten people before every decision — none of them will live your life for you, but you've handed them the steering wheel anyway
variations: Short: your happiness — why are you asking someone else?
Advanced: your life becomes a room where everyone has a vote, except you

items.sourceNote: Netflix《莎拉的真偽人生》2026春節討論度爆棚，主題「真相與謊言」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
《莎拉的真偽人生》最被截圖的一句：

「人們，

常常
把自己相信的，

當成
真相。」

他不是被騙——
他是自己選擇
要相信。

因為相信
比較舒服，
因為相信
比較不用面對，
因為相信
他就不用承認
自己其實
早就看見了。

後來騙局拆穿，
他說：「我被騙得好慘。」

沒有。
你只是
終於肯睜開眼
看你早就看到的東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明察覺了卻假裝沒看見的朋友——很多時候我們不是被騙，是自己選擇了那個比較好過的版本
variations: 短版：人們常常把自己相信的，當成真相
進階版：你不是被騙，你只是終於肯睜開眼看你早就看到的東西

en.content:
The most screenshotted line
from 'The Art of Sarah':

"People

often mistake
what they choose to believe

for
the truth."

He wasn't deceived —
he chose
to believe.

Because believing
is more comfortable,
because believing
means not having to face it,
because believing
means not admitting
he had already seen
everything.

Later, when the lie collapses,
he says: "I was so badly fooled."

No.
You just
finally opened your eyes
to what you'd already seen.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who noticed everything but pretended not to — most of the time we aren't deceived, we just pick the version that lets us sleep better
variations: Short: people often mistake what they choose to believe for the truth
Advanced: you weren't fooled — you just finally opened your eyes to what you'd already seen

items.sourceNote: 《莎拉的真偽人生》台詞「蒙蔽人眼的不是謊言，而是信任」現代友情主題改編

zh-tw.content:
最近被瘋傳的一句：

「蒙蔽人眼的

不是
謊言，

是
信任。」

陌生人騙你，
你會查證、會起疑、會比對、會去問。

但你信的人騙你——

他講什麼
你都點頭，
他改口
你還幫他找理由，
他露出破綻
你還說「他不是那種人」。

所以最後
受傷最深的
從來不是
被陌生人騙的人。

是
被自己
最信的人
拿信任
當刀的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給那個被最好朋友背叛過的你——陌生人騙不了你，因為你根本沒打開過門
variations: 短版：蒙蔽人眼的不是謊言，是信任
進階版：受傷最深的，是被自己最信的人拿信任當刀的人

en.content:
The line everyone's resharing:

"What blinds you

isn't
the lie.

It's
the trust."

When a stranger lies to you,
you check, you doubt, you cross-reference, you ask around.

But when someone you trust lies —

whatever they say,
you nod.
When their story shifts,
you help them rewrite it.
When the cracks show,
you say "that's not who they are."

Which is why
the deepest wound
never comes from
a stranger's lie.

It comes from
the person
you trusted most
using that trust
as a blade.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's been betrayed by a best friend — strangers can't trick you because you never opened the door for them in the first place
variations: Short: what blinds you isn't the lie, it's the trust
Advanced: the deepest wound comes from the person you trusted most using that trust as a blade

items.sourceNote: 《莎拉的真偽人生》「標籤夠華麗就沒人在意內容」社群比較主題原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡那句被狂截圖的：

「只要
標籤
夠華麗，

就沒人
在意
內容。」

你的工作頭銜夠長——
沒人問你做不做得開心。
你的伴侶條件夠亮眼——
沒人問你們相處有沒有累。
你的房子地段夠好——
沒人問你貸款還幾年。
你的生活照夠美——
沒人問你那天有沒有哭。

大家
只看標籤，

你也
只給標籤。

久了
你自己
都搞不清楚——

你是
活著，

還是
在維持
一個別人羨慕的
版本。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那個發限動前要修圖二十分鐘的朋友——大家只看標籤，連你都開始只給標籤
variations: 短版：只要標籤夠華麗，就沒人在意內容
進階版：你不是活著，你是在維持一個別人羨慕的版本

en.content:
The K-drama line
everyone keeps screenshotting:

"As long as
the label
is fancy enough,

no one
cares
about the content."

Your job title is long enough —
no one asks if you enjoy it.
Your partner looks good enough on paper —
no one asks if it's exhausting.
Your apartment is in the right neighborhood —
no one asks how many years left on the mortgage.
Your photos are pretty enough —
no one asks if you cried that day.

Everyone
only reads the label.

And you
only post the label.

Eventually
even you
can't tell —

are you
living,

or are you
maintaining
a version of yourself
that other people
envy.
en.editorNote: For the friend who spends twenty minutes editing a single story — everyone only reads the label, and now even you only post the label
variations: Short: as long as the label is fancy enough, no one cares about the content
Advanced: you're not living — you're maintaining a version of yourself that other people envy

items.sourceNote: 韓劇《今生也請多指教》台詞「大家都是裝出來的」職場成人主題現代改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句太真：

「人到死

都不會
真的懂事，

大家
都是
裝出來的。」

你看主管開會講得頭頭是道——
他其實也在猜。
你看前輩處理客訴游刃有餘——
他下班路上一樣崩潰。
你看爸媽好像什麼都會——
他們只是不敢在你面前慌。

你以為
別人都長大了，
只有你
還在裝。

錯了——

所有人
都在裝，

只是
有些人
裝得
比你久。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得自己永遠不夠成熟的朋友——其實大家都在裝，只是裝得比你久
variations: 短版：人到死都不會真的懂事，大家都是裝出來的
進階版：你以為只有你還在裝，其實所有人都在裝，只是有些人裝得比你久

en.content:
A K-drama line that hit too hard:

"No one

ever really
figures it out,

even by the end.

Everyone
is just
pretending."

Your manager runs the meeting smoothly —
he's guessing too.
The senior handles the angry client like a pro —
she still cries on the commute home.
Your parents seem to know everything —
they just learned not to panic in front of you.

You think
everyone grew up,
and only you
are still pretending.

Wrong —

everyone
is pretending,

some people
have just been
pretending
longer
than you have.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who feels like the only adult still faking it — everyone's faking it, some people have just been faking it longer
variations: Short: no one ever really figures it out — everyone is just pretending
Advanced: you think only you are still pretending; actually everyone is, some just longer than you

items.sourceNote: 2026 韓劇療癒系金句，主題「等待」與自我價值，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
最近被瘋傳的一句：

「不要

把
等待，

誤會成
愛。」

他說再給他一點時間——
你等。
他說現在還不是時候——
你等。
他說等他忙完這陣子——
你還是等。

你以為
等得越久，
證明你越愛。

但真相是——

會讓你
一直等的人，

從來
沒打算
讓你
等到。

愛你的人
不會
讓你
站在原地
猜
他到底
來不來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是在等一個人「準備好」的朋友——會讓你一直等的人，從來沒打算讓你等到
variations: 短版：不要把等待，誤會成愛
進階版：愛你的人不會讓你站在原地，猜他到底來不來

en.content:
The line everyone's resharing:

"Don't

mistake
waiting

for
love."

He says give him a little more time —
you wait.
He says now isn't the right moment —
you wait.
He says once this busy stretch is over —
you wait some more.

You think
the longer you wait,
the more it proves you love him.

But the truth is —

someone who keeps you
waiting

never
intended
for the wait
to end.

Someone who loves you
won't leave you
standing in one spot
guessing
whether he's
coming at all.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend always waiting for someone to be 'ready' — the person who keeps you waiting never planned for the wait to end
variations: Short: don't mistake waiting for love
Advanced: someone who loves you won't leave you standing there guessing whether he's coming

items.sourceNote: 2026 台劇親情主題金句，「父母與子女」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
台劇裡那句讓全場安靜的：

「我們

總是
對最親的人，

用
最差的
語氣。」

對同事客氣，
對朋友耐心，
對陌生人都記得說謝謝。

回到家——

媽多問一句
你就嫌煩，
爸關心一下
你就不耐，
那個最不會離開你的人，
反而
承接了
你一整天
累積的脾氣。

因為你知道
他們
不會走，

所以你
把最壞的自己
留給了
最不該
受傷的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個對外人總是很好、回家卻沒耐心的你——我們把最壞的自己，留給了最不該受傷的人
variations: 短版：我們總是對最親的人，用最差的語氣
進階版：因為你知道他們不會走，所以你把最壞的自己留給了他們

en.content:
The drama line
that silenced the whole room:

"We always

save
our worst tone

for the people
closest
to us."

Polite to coworkers,
patient with friends,
you even thank strangers without thinking.

Then you get home —

Mom asks one extra question
and you snap.
Dad checks in on you
and you sigh.
The person least likely to leave
ends up absorbing
a whole day's
built-up irritation.

Because you know
they won't go,

you hand
your worst self
to the people
who deserve
to be hurt
least.
en.editorNote: For the version of you that's lovely to outsiders but impatient at home — we save our worst self for the people who deserve it least
variations: Short: we always save our worst tone for the people closest to us
Advanced: because you know they won't leave, you hand them your worst self

items.sourceNote: 2026 韓劇職場主題金句，「努力與被看見」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡有一句太現實：

「沒被看見的

努力，

不是
沒有
價值，

是
還沒
遇到
對的人
看。」

你加的班
沒人記得，
你補的洞
沒人知道，
你扛下的事
換來一句
「這不是本來就該做的嗎」。

你開始懷疑——
是不是
努力
根本
沒用。

不是。

是你
一直
在一個
看不見你的地方
發光。

換個地方——

同樣的你，
會被
當成
寶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個努力卻一直不被看見的朋友——不是你不夠好，是你在一個看不見你的地方發光
variations: 短版：沒被看見的努力，不是沒價值，是還沒遇到對的人看
進階版：換個地方，同樣的你，會被當成寶

en.content:
A K-drama line that's painfully real:

"Effort

that goes
unseen

isn't
worthless —

it just
hasn't met
the right person
to see it
yet."

The overtime
no one remembers,
the gaps
you quietly filled,
the weight you carried
repaid with
'wasn't that just your job?'

You start to wonder —
maybe
trying
doesn't
matter
at all.

It does.

You've just been
shining
in a place
that can't
see you.

Somewhere else —

the same you
would be
treasured.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend whose effort keeps going unnoticed — it's not that you're not good enough, you're shining where no one can see you
variations: Short: unseen effort isn't worthless — it just hasn't met the right person to see it
Advanced: somewhere else, the same you would be treasured

items.sourceNote: 2026 韓劇友情主題金句，「漸行漸遠」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
最近被狂截圖的一句：

「有些朋友

不是
吵架
才散的，

是
慢慢
沒話
才散的。」

沒有誰對不起誰，
沒有翻臉，
沒有刪好友。

只是——

他有了新的生活圈，
你有了新的煩惱，
訊息
從
秒回
變成
已讀，
從
已讀
變成
不讀。

你們
不是
絕交，

是
剛好
走到了
人生
不同的
路口。

有些人
只能
陪你
走一段，

那一段
還是
真的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個曾經無話不談、現在卻不知道從何說起的老朋友——有些人只能陪你走一段，但那一段還是真的
variations: 短版：有些朋友不是吵架才散的，是慢慢沒話才散的
進階版：你們不是絕交，是剛好走到了人生不同的路口

en.content:
The line everyone keeps screenshotting:

"Some friendships

don't end
in a fight.

They end
in a slow
running
out of
things to say."

No one wronged anyone,
no falling out,
no unfollowing.

It's just —

he found a new circle,
you found new worries,
messages
went
from
instant replies
to
left on read,
from
left on read
to
never opened.

You didn't
cut each other off.

You simply
reached
different
forks
in life.

Some people
only walk
part of the way
with you,

and that part
was still
real.
en.editorNote: Send to the old friend you once told everything to but now don't know where to begin — some people only walk part of the way with you, and that part was still real
variations: Short: some friendships don't end in a fight — they end in a slow running out of things to say
Advanced: you didn't cut each other off, you just reached different forks in life

items.sourceNote: 2026 台劇自我成長主題金句，「討好別人」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
台劇裡那句點醒人的：

「你

那麼
怕
讓人
失望，

結果
最
失望
你的，

是
你
自己。」

別人開口你不會拒絕，
別人有事你第一個答應，
別人不開心你急著道歉——
哪怕
根本
不是
你的錯。

你把每個人
都照顧好了，

只有
那個
累到
說不出話的你，

沒人
問
一句
累不累。

你可以
善良，

但
別
善良到
把自己
弄丟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個永遠把別人放第一、卻把自己放最後的朋友——你可以善良，但別善良到把自己弄丟
variations: 短版：你那麼怕讓人失望，結果最失望你的，是你自己
進階版：你把每個人都照顧好了，只有那個累到說不出話的你，沒人問一句累不累

en.content:
The drama line
that snaps you awake:

"You're

so afraid
of letting
people down,

the one
most
let down
by you,

is
you."

You never say no when asked,
you're first to volunteer,
you rush to apologize when someone's upset —
even
when it
wasn't
your fault.

You took care
of everyone,

and the only one
too drained
to speak —

that version of you —

no one
asked
if you were
tired.

You can be
kind,

but don't be
so kind
you lose
yourself.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who always puts everyone first and themselves dead last — you can be kind, just don't be so kind you lose yourself
variations: Short: you're so afraid of letting people down, the one most let down by you is you
Advanced: you took care of everyone; the only one no one asked about was you

items.sourceNote: 2026 治癒系韓劇金句主題，「平凡生活」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
最近被瘋傳的韓劇台詞：

「長大
以後
才發現，

光是
把
平凡的
日子
過下去，

就
已經
用盡
全力。」

沒有升遷，
沒有大成就，
沒有讓人羨慕的人生——

只是
每天
準時
起床，
好好
吃飯，
按時
睡覺。

承認
自己
只想
當個
普通人，

一點
都
不
丟臉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總覺得自己「不夠成功」而焦慮的朋友——光是把平凡的日子過好，就已經很厲害了
variations: 短版：光是把平凡的日子過下去，就已經用盡全力
進階版：承認自己只想當個普通人，一點都不丟臉

en.content:
The K-drama line going around lately:

"It's only
as an adult
you realize —

just
keeping
an ordinary
life
going

already
takes
everything
you've got."

No promotion,
no big wins,
no life anyone envies —

just
waking up
on time,
eating properly,
going to bed
when you should.

Admitting
you only
want to be
an ordinary person

is
nothing
to be
ashamed of.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's anxious about not being 'successful enough' — just keeping an ordinary life running is already an achievement
variations: Short: just keeping an ordinary life going already takes everything you've got
Advanced: there's no shame in admitting you only want to be an ordinary person

items.sourceNote: 《淚之女王》婚姻主題金句，「等待」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡關於「等待」的一句話，戳中很多人：

「如果
你一直
在等
一個人，

等到
連
自己
都
覺得
可悲，

那
就
不是
愛了，

是
執著。」

愛
會
讓你
變得
更好，

執著
只會
讓你
一點一點
弄丟
自己。

分得清
這兩件事，
你
才
真正
長大。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在等一個不會回頭的人的朋友——愛讓你變好，執著只會讓你弄丟自己
variations: 短版：如果等一個人等到連自己都覺得可悲，那就不是愛了，是執著
進階版：愛會讓你變得更好，執著只會讓你一點一點弄丟自己

en.content:
A drama line about waiting that hits a lot of people:

"If you've been
waiting
for someone

until even
you
feel
pathetic
about it,

then
it isn't
love
anymore.

It's
attachment."

Love
makes you
better.

Attachment
just makes you
lose
yourself,
piece
by piece.

Knowing
the difference
is what
growing up
really means.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend still waiting on someone who's never coming back — love makes you better, attachment just makes you lose yourself
variations: Short: if waiting for someone makes even you feel pathetic, it's not love — it's attachment
Advanced: love makes you better; attachment just makes you lose yourself piece by piece

items.sourceNote: 《戀愛實用指南》2026 主題金句，「變老 vs. 變成熟」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡這句，把「變老」講得很可愛：

「可不可以
別
說我
變老了？

可以
說我
變得
更
成熟、
更
有
智慧
了
嗎？」

白頭髮
不叫
變老，
叫
資歷；

眼角的
細紋
不叫
變老，
叫
笑
過
的
證據。

年齡
只是
數字，

你
怎麼
看
自己，
才
是
真的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一過生日就哀號「我老了」的朋友——那不叫變老，叫變得更有智慧
variations: 短版：可不可以別說我變老？說我變得更成熟、更有智慧好嗎？
進階版：眼角的細紋不叫變老，叫笑過的證據

en.content:
This K-drama line makes getting older sound adorable:

"Could you
not
call it
getting old?

Could you
call it
becoming
more mature,
more
wise
instead?"

Gray hairs
aren't aging —
they're
seniority;

the lines
by your eyes
aren't aging —
they're
proof
you
laughed.

Age
is just
a number.

How
you see
yourself —
that's
what's
real.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who groans 'I'm so old' every birthday — that's not aging, that's leveling up in wisdom
variations: Short: could you not call it getting old? Call it becoming wiser instead
Advanced: the lines by your eyes aren't aging — they're proof you laughed

items.sourceNote: 《魷魚遊戲》信任主題金句，「相信是一種選擇」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡關於「信任」的一段，越想越有道理：

「人
會
相信
一個人，

不是
因為
那個人
值得
相信，

是
因為
他們
相信，

沒有
別人
可以
依靠
了。」

信任
從來
不是
別人
給你
的
保證，

是
你
自己
做
的
選擇。

你
要是
關上
心門，
誰
都
進
不來，

你
就
只能
一個人
站
在
世界
中間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被傷過、從此不敢再相信任何人的朋友——信任是選擇，關上心門誰都進不來
variations: 短版：信任不是別人給你的保證，是你自己做的選擇
進階版：你要是關上心門，誰都進不來，就只能一個人站在世界中間

en.content:
A K-drama take on trust that makes more sense the more you sit with it:

"People
don't trust
someone

because
that person
is worth
trusting.

They trust
because
they believe

there's
no one
else
left
to lean on."

Trust
was never
a guarantee
someone
handed you.

It's
a choice
you
make.

Close
the door
to your heart
and no one
gets in —

and you're
left
standing
in the middle
of the world
alone.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who got hurt and now refuses to trust anyone — trust is a choice; close that door and no one gets in
variations: Short: trust isn't a guarantee someone hands you — it's a choice you make
Advanced: close the door to your heart and you're left standing in the world alone

items.sourceNote: 《八尺門的辯護人》父子對話金句，「站在陸地上才知道自己在搖晃」原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
台劇裡這句，後勁很強：

「討海
的人
都是
拚命
的，

你
能
比
他們
還
狠
嗎？

只有
站
在
陸地
上，

才
知道
自己
一直
在
搖晃。」

你
以為
自己
很
穩，

是
因為
你
沒
離開
過
那片
海。

真正
看清
自己，

往往
是
停
下來
的
那
一刻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一直往前衝、從沒停下來看自己的朋友——只有停下來，才知道自己一直在搖晃
variations: 短版：只有站在陸地上，才知道自己一直在搖晃
進階版：你以為自己很穩，是因為你沒離開過那片海

en.content:
This line from a Taiwanese drama lingers:

"People
who work
the sea
all fight
for their lives —

could you
ever be
as
relentless
as them?

It's only
when you
stand
on land

that you
realize
you'd been
swaying
the whole
time."

You think
you're
steady —

but only
because
you never
left
that sea.

The moment
you truly
see
yourself

is usually
the moment
you
stop.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps charging ahead and never pauses to look at themselves — only when you stop do you realize you'd been swaying all along
variations: Short: it's only when you stand on land that you realize you'd been swaying the whole time
Advanced: you think you're steady only because you never left that sea

items.sourceNote: 韓劇治癒系金句「你的幸福為什麼要問別人」延伸改寫，原創

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡這句，看完愣了好久：

「你
的
幸福，

為什麼
要
問
別人？」

你
滑
別人的
限動，

比
較
誰
過得
比較好，

然後
回頭
覺得
自己
的
生活
哪裡
不夠。

可是
別人
的
答案，

從來
不是
你
的
題目。

你
過得
好不好，

只有
你
自己
說了
算。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老是拿別人的生活來折磨自己的朋友——你的幸福，從來不該由別人來打分數
variations: 短版：你的幸福，為什麼要問別人？
進階版：別人的答案，從來不是你的題目

en.content:
A K-drama line that left me staring at the screen:

"Your
happiness —

why
are you
asking
someone
else?"

You scroll
through
their stories,

comparing
who's
living
better,

then turn back
and decide
your own life
falls
short.

But
someone
else's
answer

was never
your
question.

Whether
you're
doing okay —

only
you
get to
say.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who keeps measuring their life against everyone else's — your happiness was never theirs to grade
variations: Short: your happiness — why are you asking someone else?
Advanced: someone else's answer was never your question

items.sourceNote: 《淚之女王》婚姻與愛情金句延伸，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡關於愛情的這段，越長大越懂：

「真正
的
喜歡，

不是
他
有
多
完美，

是
你
在
他
面前，

可以
當
那個
不
完美
的
自己。」

你
不用
裝，

不用
撐，

不用
隨時
保持
最好
的
樣子。

累
了
可以
說
累，

哭
了
不用
解釋。

那
才是
愛
讓人
安心
的
地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在感情裡一直很累、總覺得要表現完美才會被愛的人——對的人面前，你可以不完美
variations: 短版：真正的喜歡，是你在他面前可以當那個不完美的自己
進階版：累了可以說累，哭了不用解釋，那才是愛讓人安心的地方

en.content:
A K-drama take on love that hits harder the older you get:

"Real
love

isn't
about
how
perfect
they are.

It's
that
around
them

you get
to be
the
imperfect
version
of
yourself."

No
performing.

No
holding
it
together.

No
staying
at your
best
every
second.

Tired?
You can
say so.

Crying?
No
explanation
needed.

That's
where
love
feels
like
safety.
en.editorNote: Send to the person who's exhausted in their relationship, always thinking they have to be perfect to be loved — with the right person, you get to be imperfect
variations: Short: real love is being able to be the imperfect version of yourself around them
Advanced: tired? you can say so. crying? no explanation needed. that's where love feels like safety

items.sourceNote: 台劇《影后》自我反思金句「我從來沒問過自己還有沒有電」延伸，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
台劇《影后》這句，後勁很強：

「我
一直
忙著
充
別人
的
電，

卻
從來
沒
問過
自己，

還
有沒有
電。」

你
隨時
在線，

誰
找
你
都
秒回，

誰
累
了
你
都
撐
著
聽。

可是
夜
深
的
時候，

沒人
問
你
一句
還
好
嗎。

記得
先
把
自己
充
滿，

你
才
有
力氣
照亮
別人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總在照顧所有人、卻忘了照顧自己的朋友——先充滿自己，才有力氣照亮別人
variations: 短版：我一直忙著充別人的電，卻從來沒問過自己還有沒有電
進階版：先把自己充滿，你才有力氣照亮別人

en.content:
This line from a Taiwanese drama lingers:

"I've
been
so busy
charging
everyone
else,

I never
once
asked
myself

whether
I had
any
battery
left."

You're
always
online,

replying
the second
anyone
reaches
out,

holding
space
for
everyone
who's
tired.

But
late
at night,

no one
asks
if
you're
okay.

Charge
yourself
first —

only then
do you
have
the power
to light
anyone
else.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who takes care of everyone but forgets themselves — charge yourself first, then you'll have the power to light up others
variations: Short: I was so busy charging everyone else, I never asked if I had any battery left
Advanced: charge yourself first — only then do you have the power to light up others

items.sourceNote: 韓劇《今生也請多指教》「人到死都不會懂事大家都是裝出來的」延伸改寫，原創

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡這句，笑著笑著就懂了：

「人
到
死
都
不會
真的
懂事，

大家
都
只是
裝
得
很
懂
而已。」

你
以為
長大
就
會
有
答案，

結果
三十
了
還是
邊
做
邊
慌。

你
以為
別人
都
很
從容，

其實
他們
也
在
偷偷
查
怎麼
辦。

沒人
真的
準備
好，

大家
都
是
硬著
頭皮
往
前
走。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老覺得自己「還沒長大」而焦慮的朋友——沒人真的準備好，大家都是裝的
variations: 短版：人到死都不會真的懂事，大家都只是裝得很懂而已
進階版：你以為別人都很從容，其實他們也在偷偷查怎麼辦

en.content:
A K-drama line you laugh at until it lands:

"No one
ever
really
grows up,
not even
at the
end —

everyone's
just
pretending
they've
got it
figured
out."

You thought
growing up
meant
having
the
answers,

and here
you are
at thirty,
still
panicking
mid-task.

You thought
everyone
else
was
composed —

turns out
they're
also
secretly
googling
what
to do.

Nobody's
actually
ready.

We're
all
just
winging
it
forward.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who's anxious about still not feeling 'grown up' — nobody's actually ready, everyone's just pretending
variations: Short: no one ever really grows up — everyone's just pretending they've got it figured out
Advanced: you thought everyone else was composed — turns out they're also secretly googling what to do

items.sourceNote: 《請回答吧》系列家人主題金句延伸，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇《請回答吧》這段，看一次哭一次：

「家人
之間
不
說
抱歉，

也
不
說
謝謝，

因為
那些
話
太
見外。」

他們
不會
說
我
愛
你，

只會
問
你
吃
飽
沒。

他們
不會
說
我
擔心，

只會
唸
你
怎麼
這麼
晚
回。

那些
說
不
出口
的
話，

全
藏
在
一桌
還
熱
著
的
飯
裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個跟家人不太會說愛、卻彼此都懂的人——說不出口的話，都藏在一桌熱飯裡
variations: 短版：家人之間不說抱歉也不說謝謝，因為那些話太見外
進階版：那些說不出口的話，全藏在一桌還熱著的飯裡

en.content:
This bit from the K-drama Reply series — I cry every single time:

"Family
doesn't
say
sorry,

and
doesn't
say
thank
you,

because
those words
feel
too
formal
between
them."

They won't
say
'I love
you' —

they'll
ask
if you've
eaten.

They won't
say
'I was
worried' —

they'll
scold you
for
coming
home
so late.

All the
words
they can't
say

are hidden
in a table
of food
they kept
warm
for you.
en.editorNote: Send to someone whose family never says 'I love you' out loud but somehow both of you know — the unsaid words are hidden in a table of food kept warm for you
variations: Short: family doesn't say sorry or thank you — those words feel too formal between them
Advanced: all the words they can't say are hidden in a table of food they kept warm for you

items.sourceNote: 2026治癒系韓劇金句「平凡生活已耗盡全力」延伸，原創改寫

zh-tw.content:
韓劇裡這句，戳中所有撐著的人：

「長大之後才發現，
光是把日子過得普通，
就已經用盡全力了。」

以前覺得平凡很無聊，
現在覺得平凡很難。

能準時起床、好好吃飯、
不對誰發脾氣，
已經是今天的小勝利。

承認自己只想當個普通人，
一點都不丟臉。

你不是不夠努力，
你只是已經很努力很久了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總覺得自己「不夠成功」而自責的朋友——把普通的日子過下去，本身就很不容易
variations: 短版：光是把日子過得普通，就已經用盡全力了
進階版：你不是不夠努力，你只是已經很努力很久了

en.content:
A K-drama line that hits everyone who's just barely holding on:

"It's only after growing up
that you realize —
